mission.title: keda: Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling
mission.description: Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

Enhance the Kafka Scaler or create a new KEDA Scaler to support scaling Kafka by partitions per pod as opposed to simple increase in pod count.

### Use-Case

Say I have 12 partitions for my topic.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Issue: https://github.com/kedacore/keda/issues/2581
Enhance the Kafka Scaler or create a new KEDA Scaler to support scaling Kafka by partitions per pod as opposed to simple increase in pod count.

Relates to #

QA:
Tested this locally by pushing an image on a local kind cluster.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
